Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password, medical/health insurance info, username/email plus password or security Q&A, unique biometric identifiers (if unencrypted).
Notification to AG: Required if more than 1,000 residents notified; same timing as individuals (within 45 days).
Penalty: Up to $5,000 per day for delay, maximum $500,000 per breach; enforced by Attorney General as unlawful trade practice; no private right of action.

Definition_of _PII: First name or first initial and last name plus SSN, drivers license or state ID number, or financial/credit/debit account number with required password, PIN, or access code. Includes passwords or access codes that permit access to an account. Encrypted or redacted data is excluded unless the encryption key was accessed.
Notification to AG: Required if the entity decides not to notify individuals because it determined the breach is unlikely to cause harm.
Penalty: Civil penalty up to $500 per resident not notified, capped at $50,000 per incident. Violations may also be treated as unfair or deceptive acts under Alaska law with potential additional remedies.

Definition_of _PII: An individual's first name or first initial and last name in combination with one or more specified data elements. An individual's user name or e-mail address, in combination with a password or security question and answer, that allows access to an online account. Does not include publicly available information that is lawfully made available to the general public from federal, state or local government records or widely distributed media.
Notification to AG: within 45 days after the determination that more than 1000 individuals must be notifiyed, along with the the Arizona dept of homeland security head and the 3 largest national consumer reporting agencies.
Penalty: The attorney general may impose a civil penalty for a violation of this article not to exceed the lesser of $10,000 per affected individual or the total amount of economic loss sustained by affected individuals, but the maximum civil penalty from a breach or series of related breaches may not exceed $500,000.

Definition_of _PII: Expanded to include name plus SSN, DL/state ID number, financial account info, biometric data, medical info, health insurance numbers, online account credentials (unencrypted/unredacted)
Notification to AG: Must notify AG if more than 1,000 residents affected, at the same time as notice to consumers or within 45 days of determining likelihood of harm
Penalty: AG may bring action under Deceptive Trade Practices Act; civil penalties, private remedies possible

Definition_of _PII: First name or first initial and last name plus SSN, drivers license number, California ID number, or account number with required password or access code. Does not include data that is encrypted or redacted.
Notification to AG: Required if more than 500 residents must be notified. A sample of the consumer notice must be submitted to the Attorney General.
Penalty: Individuals may bring a civil action if they are injured. The Attorney General may also take action for violations.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial + last name with: SSN; student/military/passport ID; driver/ID; medical information; health insurance ID; biometric data; username/email + password or security Q&A; or financial account/credit/debit number with code/password.
Notification to AG: Notify Colorado Attorney General within 30 days if 500+ residents affected.
Penalty: Civil penalties possible.

Definition_of _PII: An individual's first name or first initial and last name in combination with one or more of the following: Social Security number; Driver's license number or state ID card number; Financial account number (e.g., credit/debit card) in combination with any required security code, access code, or password; Medical information or health insurance information; Biometric data; Taxpayer ID number; Username or email address in combination with a password or security question/answer.
Notification to AG: Must be provided "not later than the time when notice is provided to the resident." The AG has an online portal for this.
Penalty: A violation of the breach notification law is considered an **unfair trade practice** and is enforceable by the Attorney General under the Connecticut Unfair Trade Practices Act (CUTPA).

Definition_of _PII: Personal information means first name or first initial and last name combined with Social Security number, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license or state identification number, or account, credit, or debit card number with any required security or access code
Notification to AG: Must notify the Attorney General if residents are notified
Penalty: Attorney General can seek enforcement; penalties may be imposed for non-compliance

Definition_of _PII: First name or initial and last name, in combination with SSN; driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license/state ID; passport; financial, medical, or health insurance info; email/username plus password or security Q&A; biometric data (if not encrypted).
Notification to AG: Required if breach affects more than 500 residents; written notice by 30 days including synopsis, number affected, services offered, contact info, and copy of notice to individuals.
Penalty: Civil penalties up to $500,000 for violations; Attorney General enforcement; potential unfair/deceptive trade practice liability.

Definition_of _PII: First name or initial and last name in combination with any of: SSN, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license/state ID, account/credit/debit card numbers (with access codes/password), account password or PIN; or any of those elements alone if sufficient for ID theft. Applies only if unencrypted or unredacted.
Notification to AG: No explicit requirement for AG notice; but other regulatory notice may apply for specific regulated entities.
Penalty: Unfair/deceptive practice enforceable by the Attorney General; no private right of action; penalties as determined under GA Fair Business Practices Act.

Definition_of _PII: First name or first initial and last name plus SSN, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license number, Hawaii ID card number, or account, credit, or debit card number with access code or password that permits access to a financial account. Does not include publicly available information from government records. Encrypted or redacted data is excluded unless the key or process is also acquired.
Notification to AG: If a business notifies more than 1,000 persons at one time, it must, without unreasonable delay, notify the State of Hawaii Office of Consumer Protection of the timing, distribution, and content of the consumer notice.
Penalty: Businesses may face civil penalties up to 2,500 dollars per violation and are liable for actual damages to injured persons. Actions may be brought by the Attorney General or Office of Consumer Protection. Injured individuals can also bring civil actions and may recover attorneysГўв‚¬в„ў fees. Penalties are in addition to other remedies under Hawaii law.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial + last name with: SSN; driver/ID number; account/credit/debit number with required code/password.
Notification to AG: Public agencies must notify AG within 24 hours of discovery. No AG notice required for private entities.
Penalty: Civil penalties available.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password, medical/health insurance info, username/email plus password or security Q&A, unique biometric identifiers (if unencrypted).
Notification to AG: Notice shall be made in the most expedient time possible and without unreasonable delay.
Penalty: Any person, including but not limited to a third party referenced in subsection (c), who violates this Section is subject to a civil penalty of not more than $100 for each individual with respect to whom personal information is disposed of in violation of this Section. A civil penalty may not, however, exceed $50,000 for each instance of improper disposal of materials containing personal information. The Attorney General may impose a civil penalty after notice to the person accused of violating this Section and an opportunity for that person to be heard in the matter. The Attorney General may file a civil action in the circuit court to recover any penalty imposed under this Section.Г‚ Г‚ Г‚ Г‚ (e) In addition to the authority to impose a civil penalty under subsection (d), the Attorney General may bring an action in the circuit court to remedy a violation of this Section, seeking any appropriate relief.Г‚ Г‚ Г‚ Г‚ (f) A financial institution under 15 U.S.C. 6801 et. seq. or any person subject to 15 U.S.C. 1681w is exempt from this Section.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password
Notification to AG: Notice shall be made without unreasonable delay.
Penalty: Not specified

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password, username/email plus password or security Q&A, unique biometric identifiers (if unencrypted).
Notification to AG: Notice shall be made in the most expeditious manner possible and without unreasonable delay.
Penalty: The attorney general shall enforce this section

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password
Notification to AG: Notice shall be made in the most expeditious manner possible and without unreasonable delay.
Penalty: For violations of this section, except as to insurance companies licensed to do business in this state, the attorney general is empowered to bring an action in law or equity to address violations of this section and for other relief that may be appropriate.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password
Notification to AG: Not specified
Penalty: Not specified

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password
Notification to AG: Notice shall be made in the most expeditious manner possible and without unreasonable delay.
Penalty: A violation is treated as an **unfair or deceptive act or practice** under Mass. Gen. Laws ch. 93A. The Attorney General may bring an action to seek injunctive relief and civil penalties.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password
Notification to AG: Notice must be made without unreasonable delay after discovery of the breach.
Penalty: (h)Гўв‚¬Ж’For violations of this section by an insurance company licensed to do business in this state, the insurance commissioner shall have the sole authority to enforce the provisions of this section.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password
Notification to AG: Not Specified
Penalty: The attorney general shall enforce this section

Definition_of _PII: First name or initial and last name, in combination with (a) SSN, (b) driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license/state ID number, (c) account/credit/debit card plus password or access code. Applies only if unencrypted/unredacted.
Notification to AG: Only required if insurance-specific or >100 residdednts with senate bill 2046
Penalty: Failure to comply constitutes an unfair trade practice, enforceable by the Attorney General; no private right of action specified; possible civil penalties.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password, medical/health insurance info, username/email plus password or security Q&A
Notification to AG: Required if more than 1,000 residents notified
Penalty: The attorney general shall have exclusive authority to bring an action to obtain actual damages for a willful and knowing violation of this section and may seek a civil penalty not to exceed one hundred fifty thousand dollars per breach of the security of the system or series of breaches of a similar nature that are discovered in a single investigation.

timeline_days(individual): The disclosure must be made without unreasonable delay, consistent with the legitimate needs of law enforcement, as provided in subsection (3), or consistent with any measures necessary to determine the scope of the breach and restore the reasonable integrity of the data system.
Notification to AG: submit an electronic copy of the notification and a statement providing the date and method of distribution of the notification to the attorney general's consumer protection office, excluding any information that personally identifies any individual who is entitled to receive notification.
Penalty:
(2) In an action brought under 30-14-111, if the court finds that a person is willfully using or has willfully used a method, act, or practice declared unlawful by 30-14-103, the department, upon petition to the court, may recover on behalf of the state a civil fine of not more than $10,000 for each violation. The fine provided for in this subsection is in addition to any liability that a person might be subject to under subsection (1).
 
 (3) A person who engages in a fraudulent course of conduct declared unlawful by 30-14-103 shall upon conviction be fined an amount not more than $5,000, imprisoned for not more than 1 year, or both, in the discretion of the court. This subsection does not limit any other provision of this part.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with one or more of: SSN, DL/State ID, Financial Account # + Access Code/Password, Electronic ID/Email + Password, Health Info, Biometric Data, Military ID, Passport ID, or Tax ID.
timeline_days(individual): As soon as possible and without unreasonable delay
Notification to AG: Required: Not later than the time notice is provided to the Nebraska resident.
Penalty: Enforced by the AG; violation is an unlawful act or practice under the Consumer Protection Act.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with any one or more of: SSN, DL/ID Card #, Financial Account # + Access Code/Password, Medical/Health Insurance ID, or Username/Email + Password/Security Q&A
Notification to AG: Not explicitly required by statute. AG suggests voluntary notice via email.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with any one or more of: SSN, DL/Other Gov't ID #, or Account # + Access Code/Password.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with one or more of: SSN, DL/State ID, Account/Card # + Access Code/Password, Medical History/Treatment, or Health Insurance Info.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with one or more of: SSN, DL/State ID, Financial Account # + Access Code/Password, Unique Biometric Data, or Medical/Health Insurance Info.
Notification to AG: Mandatory

Definition_of _PII:
Private information is personal information (first name/initial and last name) + (1) social security number;
 
 (2) driver's license number or non-driver identification card number;

 that would permit access to an individual's financial account;

 a fingerprint, voice print, retina or iris image, or other unique

 which are used to authenticate or ascertain the individual's identity;
 or
 
 (6) medical information, meaning any information regarding an
 individual's medical history, mental or physical condition, or medical
 treatment or diagnosis by a health care professional; or
 
 (7) health insurance information, meaning an individual's health
 insurance policy number or subscriber identification number, any unique
 identifier used by a health insurer to identify the individual or any
 information in an individual's application and claims history, including

 (ii) a user name or e-mail address in combination with a password or

 account
Notification to AG:

 hundred residents of New York, the person or business shall provide the
 written determination to the state attorney general within ten days
 after the determination.
Penalty: Failure to notify: up to **$20 per instance** (max $250,000). Failure to maintain safeguards: up to $5,000 per violation.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with one or more of: SSN, DL/State ID, or Financial Account # + Access Code/Password.
Notification to AG: Required if notice is provided to affected consumers
Penalty: Violation of G.S. 75-1.1

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with any one of the following: SSN, DL/State ID, Financial Account # + Access Code/Password, Date of Birth, Mother's Maiden Name, or Any other data element that allows the business to physically or electronically contact the individual.
Notification to AG: Disclose to AG if breach exceeds 250 individuals
Penalty: Civil Penalties Up to $5000

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with any one or more of: SSN, DL/State ID, or Account/Card # + Access Code/Password.
Notification to AG: Not required, but must report to consumer reporting agency if more than 1000 residents affected

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name in combination with any one or more of: SSN, DL #, or Account/Card # + Access Code/Password.
Notification to AG: Not specified

Definition_of _PII: 1) First name or first initial and last name plus SSN, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license number, state ID number, passport number, or financial account number with required access code or password. 2) Includes medical information, health insurance policy numbers, and biometric data. 3) Encrypted data is excluded unless the encryption key is also acquired.
timeline_days(individual): As quickly as possible and without unreasonable delay, consistent with law enforcement requests and measures needed to determine scope or restore system integrity.
Notification to AG: Required if the breach affects more than 250 Oregon residents. A copy of the consumer notice must be provided.
Penalty: The Attorney General may bring an action for civil penalties. Penalties may reach up to 1,000 dollars per violation, with overall limits determined by Oregon consumer protection law.

Definition_of _PII: Personal information means first name or initial and last name with: Social Security number, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license or state ID number, or financial account, credit, or debit card number with any required security code or access code
timeline_days(individual): Without unreasonable delay following discovery of the breach
Notification to AG: If more than 1,000 Pennsylvania residents are notified, must also notify nationwide consumer reporting agencies
Penalty: Attorney General may enforce; civil penalties possible; direct cause of action for damages

Definition_of _PII: An individual's first name or first initial and last name in combination with one or more of the following (when not encrypted): 1) Social Security number; 2) Driver's license number or RI identification card number; 3) Account, credit, or debit card number + any required security code, access code, or password; 4) Medical information or health insurance information; 5) Email address + any required security code, access code, or password.
timeline_days(individual): "In the most expedient time possible, but no later than **forty-five (45) calendar days** after confirmation of the breach," unless delayed by law enforcement.
Notification to AG: Required if the breach affects **more than 500** Rhode Island residents. Notification to the AG must be made "without delaying notice to affected Rhode Island residents."
Penalty: The Attorney General may bring action. Гўв‚¬Вў **Reckless violation:** Civil penalty of not more than \*\*$100 per record\*\*. Гўв‚¬Вў **Knowing and willful violation:** Civil penalty of not more than **$200 per record**.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus one or more: SSN, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license/state ID, financial account/credit/debit card (with security/access code), or similar unique identifiers. Applies only if not encrypted/redacted. Includes data elements enabling access to individualГўв‚¬в„ўs accounts or unique ID numbers issued by the government.
timeline_days(individual): Most expedient time possible and without unreasonable delay, consistent with law enforcement needs, or measures to determine scope and restore system integrity.
Notification to AG: If >1,000 residents notified, must notify Department of Consumer AffairsГўв‚¬в„ў Consumer Protection Division without unreasonable delay, providing notice details.
Penalty: Administrative fine up to $1,000 per resident per violation; private right of action and AG enforcement; injunctive relief and attorney fees possible.

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial + last name with: SSN; driver/ID; account/credit/debit number with required code/password; medical info; health insurance info; biometric data; plus online account credentials.
timeline_days(individual): Most expedient time possible and without unreasonable delay, but no later than 60 days after discovery unless law enforcement delay.
Notification to AG: Notify South Dakota AG if >250 residents affected.
Penalty: Civil penalties possible.

Definition_of _PII: An individual's first name or first initial and last name, in combination with Social Security number, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license or identification card number, or account, credit, or debit card number with security code, access code, or password
timeline_days(individual): Must be made Гўв‚¬Е“immediately,Гўв‚¬Вќ no later than 45 days from discovery of the breach, unless delayed at the request of law enforcement
Notification to AG: No general statewide AG notification requirement for private sector breaches; state agencies must notify the Comptroller within 5 working days
Penalty: Civil lawsuits may be brought for failure to notify within required 45 days; AG enforcement and damages possible

Definition_of _PII: First name/initial and last name plus at least one of: SSN, driver's license/state ID, financial account number plus access code/password, medical/health insurance info, username/email plus password or security Q&A, unique biometric identifiers (if unencrypted).eleectroniic address, telecommunicatioins access device, mother's maiden name

Penalty:
(a) A person who violates this chapter is liable to this state for a civil penalty of at least $2,000 but not more than $50,000 for each violation. The attorney general may bring an action to recover the civil penalty imposed under this subsection.
 
 
 
 (a-1) In addition to penalties assessed under Subsection (a), a person who fails to take reasonable action to comply with Section 521.053(b) is liable to this state for a civil penalty of not more than $100 for each individual to whom notification is due under that subsection for each consecutive day that the person fails to take reasonable action to comply with that subsection. Civil penalties under this section may not exceed $250,000 for all individuals to whom notification is due after a single breach. Definition_of _PII: First name/initial + last name with: SSN; financial account or card number with code/password; driver/ID number; plus standalone online account credentials covered for credential notice.

Definition_of _PII: "A consumer's first name or first initial and last name in combination with one or more of the following (when not encrypted or redacted): 1) Social Security number; 2) Financial account number (e.g., credit/debit card) + any required security code, access code, or password; 3) Driver's license or state ID card number; 4) or other access code for a financial account; 5) Unique biometric data; 6) Health information or health insurance policy number; 7) Username or email address + password or security /answer."

Definition_of _PII: First name or first initial and last name (of state resident), in combination with any one or more of: SSN, driverГўв‚¬в„ўs license/state ID number, passport number, financial account/credit/debit card plus access codes/passwords, biometric data (fingerprints, retina, iris), and medical/health insurance info.
